updates.title: System Updates
updates.install_success: Update installed successfully. Please restart Shogun.
updates.install_failed: Update failed. Check logs for details.

backups.title: Backups
backups.enabled_desc: Automatically back up your database, configs, and governance files on a schedule.
backups.max_backups_desc: Older backups beyond this limit are automatically deleted.
backups.include_vector_desc: Include Qdrant vector data in backups (increases size significantly).
backups.restore_confirm: Are you sure? This will overwrite your current database and configs. A restart is required.
backups.no_backups: No backups yet. Create one or enable automatic backups.

setup.step1_subtitle: Select the language for the Shogun interface. You can change this later from the Dashboard.
setup.step1_explainer: The language you choose will be applied to all menus, labels, buttons, and system messages throughout the Shogun platform. All 14 languages have been professionally translated to cover the full interface. If you change your mind, you can switch languages at any time from the Dashboard's Quick Actions panel — no restart required.
setup.step2_subtitle: Choose where your Shogun data will be stored. This includes the database, vector memory, configurations, and logs.
setup.step2_explainer: This is the root directory where Shogun stores all persistent data. The database (shogun.db) holds agents, providers, and configuration. The vector memory (qdrant/) powers semantic search and agent recall. Configuration files (configs/) contain your constitution, mandate, and system settings. Logs (logs/) record all agent activity for audit and debugging. You can use the default path or specify a custom one — the directory will be created automatically if it doesn't exist.
setup.step3_subtitle: Define your AI's identity, personality, and decision-making parameters.
setup.step3_explainer: Your Shogun is the primary orchestrating AI agent. Its name and description define how it identifies itself. The Autonomy Level controls how independently it acts — lower values require more human approval, higher values allow more autonomous decision-making. The Tone, Risk, Verbosity, Planning, Tools, Security, and Memory parameters shape how the agent reasons, communicates, and interacts with the system. These can all be fine-tuned later from the Shogun Profile page.
setup.step4_subtitle: Define the rules and priorities that guide how your Shogun thinks and acts. These are the philosophical foundations for decision-making.
setup.step4_explainer: Behavioral directives are YAML-formatted rules that define the Shogun's operational philosophy. Priorities determine what the agent values most (e.g., safety, stealth, efficiency). Operational constraints set hard limits on resource access and verification thresholds. Delegation rules control which tasks the Shogun handles directly versus those it delegates to Samurai sub-agents. The defaults are battle-tested — edit them only if you have specific operational requirements.
setup.step5_subtitle: Add at least one AI model provider. This is where your Shogun's intelligence comes from.
setup.step5_explainer: Model providers supply the large language models (LLMs) that power your Shogun's reasoning. You can connect cloud providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google, or use local models via Ollama. Each provider needs an API key (for cloud) or a base URL (for local). You can add multiple providers for redundancy. Test the connection before proceeding to ensure the API key is valid. If you skip this step, you can configure providers later from the Katana page.
setup.step6_subtitle: The Constitution defines hard rules the AI cannot break. The Mandate defines soft directives that shape behavior.
setup.step6_explainer: The Constitution is a YAML document that defines inviolable rules enforced at the server level — the agent physically cannot bypass these. Critical rules block dangerous actions, while lesser rules audit or warn. The Mandate is a Markdown document injected into every conversation as a system prompt, guiding the agent's tone, focus, and priorities. Together, they form the governance framework that keeps your Shogun aligned with your objectives. Both can be versioned and updated later via the Kaizen page.
setup.step7_subtitle: Choose a primary model for reasoning and fallback models for resilience.
setup.step7_explainer: The primary model handles all default Shogun tasks — reasoning, planning, and conversation. Choose the most capable model from your connected providers. Fallback models activate automatically when the primary is unavailable (rate limits, outages, etc.). Order matters: the first fallback is tried first. Drag to reorder. If you haven't connected any providers yet, you can configure models later from the Shogun Profile.
setup.step8_subtitle: Review your configuration. Everything can be adjusted later from the respective pages.
setup.step8_explainer: Take a moment to review the summary below. When you click 'Rise, Shogun', the system will create your agent, register your model providers, write your constitution and mandate files, and mark the installation as complete. You'll be redirected to the Guide page to learn more about the platform. If anything looks wrong, use the Back button to adjust — nothing is saved until you confirm.
setup.step3_description_placeholder: Master orchestrator of the Samurai Network.
setup.step6_constitution_desc: YAML rules that are enforced server-side. Critical rules block actions; lower-priority rules warn or audit.
setup.step6_mandate_desc: Markdown directives injected into every conversation to guide tone, focus, and priorities.
setup.step7_primary_desc: The default model used for all Shogun reasoning and task execution.
setup.step7_fallback_desc: Used when the primary is unavailable. Order matters — drag to reorder.
setup.step7_no_providers: No providers configured. You can set up models later in the Shogun Profile.
setup.step7_no_fallbacks: No fallbacks selected — the primary model will always be used.

guide.title: Framework Guide
guide.subtitle: Master the Shogun architecture, operations, and data management.
guide.disclaimer_body: Shogun is a free to use AI agent orchestration framework. It is intended to help users build, manage, and operate autonomous AI workflows, but it does not guarantee accuracy, completeness, reliability, or suitability for any particular purpose. Shogun is provided as is, and use of the framework is entirely at the user's own risk.
guide.disclaimer_oversight: Users are responsible for ensuring appropriate human oversight and for validating all outputs, actions, and decisions produced with or through Shogun. Human review should remain in the loop at all times, particularly in legal, financial, regulatory, security, or other high-impact use cases.

torii.title: The Torii
torii.subtitle: Security gateway and access control.
torii.tier_shrine_desc: Zero-trust. Local only. No external tool execution.
torii.tier_campaign_desc: High autonomy. Broad network access. Automated spawns.
torii.emergency_desc: Activating RONIN mode or the Kill Switch removes safety gates. Use only inside a fully isolated environment.
torii.no_policies: No policies defined. Create one below.
torii.no_permissions: No explicit permissions set — inherits posture defaults.

torii.reset_success: Kill switch reset. Posture restored to TACTICAL.

bushido.title: Bushido
bushido.subtitle: Monitor and calibrate the autonomous feedback loops that optimize agent precision.
bushido.reflection_intensity_desc: Higher intensity increases token consumption but yields far deeper logical validation.
bushido.exploration_variance_desc: Controls how much the agent explores novel approaches vs. sticking to proven patterns.
bushido.heartbeat_frequency_desc: Interval in minutes for system heartbeat updates, checking cron jobs, and syncing emails.
bushido.no_recommendations: No recommendations yet. Run a Bushido job to generate insights.
bushido.formal_verification_desc: The Bushido engine uses formal verification loops to ensure all behavioral optimizations remain strictly within the boundaries defined in the Kaizen constitution.
bushido.reflection_initiated: Reflection cycle initiated. Monitor the Insight Stream for results.
